cards.back: A distributed computing framework designed to store and process large data sets
Because it can handle large amounts of data, scalable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective
Because RDBMS is not designed to handle large data sets, it can be slow and expensive
RDBMS is suitable for small to medium-sized data sets, while Hadoop is suitable for large data sets
Hadoop was first released in 2005 by Doug Cutting and Mike Cafarella
